name: Guadiamar Green Corridor Loop
name_original: Ribera Río Guadiamar — circular desde la Laguna de las Doblas
description: Jog this difficult 12.0-mile (19.4 km) circular route from Laguna de las Doblas, featuring riverside views and a wooden walkway.
region_intro: Jogging routes Sanlúcar La Mayor traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, a protected natural area featuring dense riverside forests and botanical gardens. The region also includes Mediterranean pastureland (dehesa) and fertile agricultural fields of olive groves and vineyards. Topographical variations, including ravines, add interest to the running terrain. Best jogging routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor The most popular jogging route is Guadiamar Green Corridor Loop, a 12.0 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views along the Guadiamar Green Corridor, connecting natural spaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Dehesa of the Guadiamar – Laguna de las Doblas loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This route winds through Mediterranean forest and past the Laguna de las Doblas. Local runners also love the Running loop from Benacazón, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through varied local terrain, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Sanlúcar La Mayor is defined by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, dehesa landscapes, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Sanlúcar La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The jogging trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor offer a diverse landscape. You'll find routes traversing the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013143738/guadiamar-green-corridor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor&lt;/a&gt; with its riverside forests and botanical gardens, as well as paths through Mediterranean pastureland (dehesa), fertile olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields. Some routes also feature interesting topographical variations due to ravines.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in the area?
A: Yes, Sanlúcar La Mayor offers several easy running paths perfect for beginners. While many routes are moderate, there are at least 4 routes specifically categorized as easy, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful scenery.

Q: What are some scenic landmarks or natural features I can see while running?
A: Many running routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; with its wooden walkways and Mediterranean forest, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3113504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt; and various religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7086624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Loreto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for circular running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38284407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa of the Guadiamar – Laguna de las Doblas loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that takes you through Mediterranean forest and past the Laguna de las Doblas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14617967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa of the Guadiamar – Dehesa de Guadiamar loop from Parking Las Doblas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The running routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013143738/guadiamar-green-corridor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.0-mile (19.4 km) trail offering extensive views. The region also hosts events like the 'Carrera de Fondo Guadiamar Sanlúcar la Mayor' with 15 km and 30 km circuits, indicating the availability of longer routes.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38284407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa of the Guadiamar – Laguna de las Doblas loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby lakes and reservoirs include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cota de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or amenities near the running trails?
A: Yes, you can find amenities near some of the trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7389130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doblas Recreation Area - El Cardenal&lt;/a&gt; is a good spot, and there's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a convenient stop for refreshments.

Q: What is a good moderate running route to explore the local area?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14609456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Benacazón&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.9-mile (7.9 km) trail leads through varied local terrain and is often completed in about 51 minutes, offering a good balance of distance and scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of cultural sights and natural beauty?
A: Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31618088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María – Dehesa of the Guadiamar loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;. This route combines the natural beauty of the Dehesa of the Guadiamar with the opportunity to pass by the historic Church of Santa María, offering a blend of cultural and natural exploration.
tips: Small bridge that crosses the Río Guadiamar within what is known as the Green Corridor of the Río Guadiamar. It's time to go with caution as the bridge is not what we know as a usual bridge!
Path that is close to the riverbed of the Río Guadiamar and where you can find animals and different species associated with the river! Quiet way not to miss it!
Visit in spring. Easily accessible by bicycle with parking for a vehicle very close.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 19.4 km long. Based on an average jogging pace, it typically takes around 2 hours and 13 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally considered easy for hiking, its length and the 'difficult' rating for jogging suggest it's best suited for those with a good level of fitness. The minimal elevation changes make it approachable, but the distance requires endurance.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ribera Rio Guadiamar circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features pressed earth paths, which are comfortable for jogging. You'll also encounter some short cemented sections at fords and a distinctive wooden walkway that extends over Laguna de las Doblas. The elevation changes are minimal, making it largely flat.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll jog through an ecosystem of riverside vegetation, including poplars, ash trees, elms, and oleanders. The area is excellent for birdwatching, especially aquatic birds around Laguna de las Doblas. You might also spot wild olive trees, lentisk bushes, and even horses grazing in open fields.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, key highlights include the beautiful Laguna de las Doblas with its wooden walkway, the scenic Bridge and meadows Río Guadiamar, and the Dehesa de Guadiamar. The trail is also part of the larger Corredor Verde del Guadiamar Protected Landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque views year-round. However, for comfortable jogging, spring and autumn are ideal, as temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Seville, so early mornings or late afternoons would be preferable if visiting then.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ribera Rio Guadiamar circular trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point at Laguna de las Doblas offers a recreational area, and you'll find parking available there, making it a convenient access point for the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Ribera Rio Guadiamar trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially around bird observation areas and where horses might be grazing.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Guadiamar Green Corridor Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Guadiamar Green Corridor Protected Landscape, including this route. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a larger network. It utilizes significant sections of the Corredor Verde trail and also intersects with paths like the Cordel de los Carboneros, Camino Real Viejo, and Cañada Real de Huelva.

faqs.question: What facilities are available at the starting point?
faqs.answer: The starting point at Laguna de las Doblas features a recreational area equipped with tables, benches, and barbecue facilities, making it a great spot for a longer outing or a post-jog picnic.
